column7: The "Brave Boys" floorball team, organized by Special Olympics Namibia, represented the country at the 2025 Special Olympics World Winter Games in Turin, Italy, where they finished fourth. Special Olympics Namibia also won two awards at the 2025 Annual National Sport Awards.
column8: The Namibia Floorball Federation does not exist as a separate entity. Floorball is managed and promoted in Namibia by Special Olympics Namibia (SON) as a sport for athletes with intellectual disabilities. SON is operational and actively participates in international competitions. The current National Director is Vilho Namufinda, and the Board Chair is Hanna Garises.

column7: The local club, Windhuckers Ultimate, is actively playing and recruiting new members as of September 2024, with regular Sunday games at Academia Secondary School Field in Windhoek.
column8: The Namibia Flying Disc Federation (NFDF) was approved as a member of the World Flying Disc Federation (WFDF) in January 2022. Ultimate is the only flying disc sport currently played in the country, with a focus on youth development and expanding the sport beyond the capital, Windhoek.

column7: Not Available. The federation is not officially recognized by World Lacrosse, and no recent activities or programs for 2024-2025 could be found. The only mention of Namibia in a lacrosse context is a historical reference to a women's hockey match against Ireland.
column8: The Namibia Lacrosse Federation appears to be a non-existent or completely nascent organization. It is not listed as a member of World Lacrosse or the Africa Association of Lacrosse. No official website, social media presence, or personnel could be identified. The sport's presence in Namibia is minimal, with no evidence of an active national federation, league, or club structure.

column7: Umpire Ewoud Lassen was selected to be part of the ICC Development Panel of Umpires in January 2025. The association was responsible for appointing umpires for the International Matches at the Namibia Open Championship in July 2025. The association also promotes umpire education and development courses.
column8: The Namibia Cricket Umpires Association is the official body responsible for the appointment, education, and development of cricket umpires and scorers in Namibia, operating under the national governing body, Cricket Namibia.
